context.pr_body:
## 📝 Summary

<!--
Provide a concise summary of what this pull request is addressing.

If this PR fixes any issues, list them here by number (e.g., Fixes #123).
-->
This pull request introduces a --force / -f flag to the marimo export subcommands. This allows users to overwrite existing output files, which is essential for automation and scripting use cases.

Fixes #5523

## 🔍 Description of Changes

<!--
Detail the specific changes made in this pull request. Explain the problem addressed and how it was resolved. If applicable, provide before and after comparisons, screenshots, or any relevant details to help reviewers understand the changes easily.
-->

Currently, when marimo export is used with an --output argument that points to an existing file, it interactively prompts the user for confirmation before overwriting. While this is a safe default for manual use, it blocks non-interactive workflows, such as in CI/CD pipelines or automated scripts.

This PR resolves the issue by:

Adding a --force and -f option to all relevant export commands (html, script, md, ipynb, and html_wasm).

Updating the watch_and_export function to check for this flag. If force is True, the prompt_to_overwrite confirmation step is skipped, and the file is overwritten directly.

Adding a new pytest test case (test_cli_export_html_force_overwrite) to ensure the new functionality works as expected. The test verifies that:

Attempting to export to an existing file without the flag does not overwrite it.

Using --force successfully overwrites the existing file.

Using the short alias -f also works correctly.

These changes make the export feature more versatile and friendly for automated environments without altering the safe default behavior for interactive users.

I know it is not perfect, please @dmadisetti @mscolnick check this one out!
## 📋 Checklist

- [x] I have read the [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/marimo-team/marimo/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [x] For large changes, or changes that affect the public API: this change was discussed or approved through an issue, on [Discord](https://marimo.io/discord?ref=pr), or the community [discussions](https://github.com/marimo-team/marimo/discussions) (Please provide a link if applicable).
- [x] I have added tests for the changes made.
- [x] I have run the code and verified that it works as expected.

agents.name: analysis_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README

A reactive Python notebook that's reproducible, git-friendly, and deployable as scripts or apps.

Docs · Discord · Examples · Gallery · YouTube

English | 简体中文 | 日本語 | Español

**marimo** is a reactive Python notebook: run a cell or interact with a UI element, and marimo automatically runs dependent cells (or marks them as stale), keeping code and outputs consistent. marimo notebooks are stored as pure Python (with first-class SQL support), executable as scripts, and deployable as apps. **Highlights**. - 🚀 **batteries-included:** replaces `jupyter`, `streamlit`, `jupytext`, `ipywidgets`, `papermill`, and more - ⚡️ **reactive**: run a cell, and marimo reactively [runs all dependent cells](https://docs.marimo.io/guides/reactivity.html) or marks them as stale - 🖐️ **interactive:** [bind sliders, tables, plots, and more](https://docs.marimo.io/guides/interactivity.html) to Python — no callbacks required - 🐍 **git-friendly:** stored as `.py` files - 🛢️ **designed for data**: query dataframes, databases, warehouses, or lakehouses [with SQL](https://docs.marimo.io/guides/working_with_data/sql.html), filter and search [dataframes](https://docs.marimo.io/guides/working_with_data/dataframes.html) - 🤖 **AI-native**: [generate cells with AI](ht ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods
agents.input_prompt:
Analyze this Pull Request:

## Title
Feat/add force capability on export

## Description
## 📝 Summary

<!--
Provide a concise summary of what this pull request is addressing.

If this PR fixes any issues, list them here by number (e.g., Fixes #123).
-->
This pull request introduces a --force / -f flag to the marimo export subcommands. This allows users to overwrite existing output files, which is essential for automation and scripting use cases.

Fixes #5523

## 🔍 Description of Changes

<!--
Detail the specific changes made in this pull request. Explain the problem addressed and how it was resolved. If applicable, provide before and after comparisons, screenshots, or any relevant details to help reviewers understand the changes easily.
-->

Currently, when marimo export is used with an --output argument that points to an existing file, it interactively prompts the user for confirmation before overwriting. While this is a safe default for manual use, it blocks non-interactive workflows, such as in CI/CD pipelines or automated scripts.

This PR resolves the issue by:

Adding a --force and -f option to all relevant export commands (html, script, md, ipynb, and html_wasm).

Updating the watch_and_export function to check for this flag. If force is True, the prompt_to_overwrite confirmation step is skipped, and the file is overwritten directly.

Adding a new pytest test case (test_cli_export_html_force_overwrite) to ensure the new functionality works as expected. The test verifies that:

Attempting to export to an existing file without the flag does not overwrite it.

Using --force successfully overwrites the existing file.

Using the short alias -f also works correctly.

These changes make the export feature more versatile and friendly for automated environments without altering the safe default behavior for interactive users.

I know it is not perfect, please @dmadisetti @mscolnick check this one out!
## 📋 Checklist

- [x] I have read the [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/marimo-team/marimo/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [x] For large changes, or changes that affect the public API: this change was discussed or approved through an issue, on [Discord](https://marimo.io/discord?ref=pr), or the community [discussions](https://github.com/marimo-team/marimo/discussions) (Please provide a link if applicable).
- [x] I have added tests for the changes made.
- [x] I have run the code and verified that it works as expected.


Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

output.pr_body:
This PR introduces a --force / -f flag to the marimo export subcommands (html, script, md, ipynb, and html_wasm) to allow overwriting existing output files in non-interactive environments (CI/CD, automation). If --force is provided, the existing file is overwritten without prompting the user. The watch_and_export flow is updated to skip the interactive overwrite prompt when force is True. A new test (test_cli_export_html_force_overwrite) is planned to validate:
- Without the flag, exporting to an existing file does not overwrite.
- With --force, the existing file is overwritten.
- The short alias -f also works.

These changes preserve the safe interactive prompt for manual users while enabling automation-friendly behavior.
output.masca_optimized:
MASCA optimization focuses on enabling a force-overwrite capability for export commands driven by CLI, which is a direct hit on the input/output subsystem where user prompts previously blocked automation. The changes touch the CLI export pathway (marimo/_cli/export/commands.py) and the common export orchestration (watch_and_export). The main goals are:
- Introduce a new boolean flag (force) across export commands: html, script, md, ipynb, html_wasm.
- Propagate the flag so that when force is True, the overwrite prompt is bypassed and files are written directly.
- Keep existing interactive safety for users who do not pass --force.
- Extend tests to cover force overwrite behavior (non-force vs. force, including short alias).

Impact analysis:
- Minimal API surface changes (new flag across commands).
- Central change in watch_and_export to honor force.
- No changes to default behavior; interactive users remain safe unless --force is provided.
- Potentially adds a new test to validate the force path.

files_to_modify.reason: Add a global --force / -f flag to export commands and plumb through to the shared export logic. Update watch_and_export to skip prompt_to_overwrite when force is True.

functions_to_modify.reason: Modify signature to include force: bool = False and bypass prompt_to_overwrite when force is True. This enables non-interactive overwrites across all export commands.
Add --force flag to the html export command and pass it to watch_and_export so overwrites can be forced in HTML exports.
Add --force flag to the script export command and propagate to watch_and_export.
Add --force flag to the md export command and propagate to watch_and_export.
Add --force flag to the ipynb export command and propagate to watch_and_export.
Add --force flag to the html_wasm export command and propagate to watch_and_export.

output.analysis_summary:
Summary of analysis:
- The PR aims to introduce a --force/-f option across all marimo export subcommands to enable overwriting existing outputs without interactive prompts, improving automation and CI/CD usability.
- The core change is to the watch_and_export function in marimo/_cli/export/commands.py, adding a force parameter and adjusting overwrite logic accordingly.
- Each export command (html, script, md, ipynb, html_wasm) must expose the --force flag and pass it into watch_and_export.
- A new test (test_cli_export_html_force_overwrite) is planned to validate behavior: non-force should not overwrite; force should overwrite; short alias -f should work.
- Potential edge considerations include consistency of behavior for directory outputs (html_wasm) and ensuring existing assets or outputs are safely overwritten when forced.
- Overall, changes are targeted, maintain backward compatibility for interactive users, and unlock automation scenarios without altering default safety.

agents.name: context_planner_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```
agents.input_prompt:
Generate an implementation plan for this PR:

## PR Title
Feat/add force capability on export

## PR Body
This PR introduces a --force / -f flag to the marimo export subcommands (html, script, md, ipynb, and html_wasm) to allow overwriting existing output files in non-interactive environments (CI/CD, automation). If --force is provided, the existing file is overwritten without prompting the user. The watch_and_export flow is updated to skip the interactive overwrite prompt when force is True. A new test (test_cli_export_html_force_overwrite) is planned to validate:
- Without the flag, exporting to an existing file does not overwrite.
- With --force, the existing file is overwritten.
- The short alias -f also works.

These changes preserve the safe interactive prompt for manual users while enabling automation-friendly behavior.

## MASCA Analysis (optimized)
MASCA optimization focuses on enabling a force-overwrite capability for export commands driven by CLI, which is a direct hit on the input/output subsystem where user prompts previously blocked automation. The changes touch the CLI export pathway (marimo/_cli/export/commands.py) and the common export orchestration (watch_and_export). The main goals are:
- Introduce a new boolean flag (force) across export commands: html, script, md, ipynb, html_wasm.
- Propagate the flag so that when force is True, the overwrite prompt is bypassed and files are written directly.
- Keep existing interactive safety for users who do not pass --force.
- Extend tests to cover force overwrite behavior (non-force vs. force, including short alias).

Impact analysis:
- Minimal API surface changes (new flag across commands).
- Central change in watch_and_export to honor force.
- No changes to default behavior; interactive users remain safe unless --force is provided.
- Potentially adds a new test to validate the force path.


## Files to Modify
- marimo/_cli/export/commands.py: Add a global --force / -f flag to export commands and plumb through to the shared export logic. Update watch_and_export to skip prompt_to_overwrite when force is True.

## Functions to Modify
- watch_and_export (marimo/_cli/export/commands.py): Modify signature to include force: bool = False and bypass prompt_to_overwrite when force is True. This enables non-interactive overwrites across all export commands.
- html (marimo/_cli/export/commands.py): Add --force flag to the html export command and pass it to watch_and_export so overwrites can be forced in HTML exports.
- script (marimo/_cli/export/commands.py): Add --force flag to the script export command and propagate to watch_and_export.
- md (marimo/_cli/export/commands.py): Add --force flag to the md export command and propagate to watch_and_export.
- ipynb (marimo/_cli/export/commands.py): Add --force flag to the ipynb export command and propagate to watch_and_export.
- html_wasm (marimo/_cli/export/commands.py): Add --force flag to the html_wasm export command and propagate to watch_and_export.

## Analysis Summary
Summary of analysis:
- The PR aims to introduce a --force/-f option across all marimo export subcommands to enable overwriting existing outputs without interactive prompts, improving automation and CI/CD usability.
- The core change is to the watch_and_export function in marimo/_cli/export/commands.py, adding a force parameter and adjusting overwrite logic accordingly.
- Each export command (html, script, md, ipynb, html_wasm) must expose the --force flag and pass it into watch_and_export.
- A new test (test_cli_export_html_force_overwrite) is planned to validate behavior: non-force should not overwrite; force should overwrite; short alias -f should work.
- Potential edge considerations include consistency of behavior for directory outputs (html_wasm) and ensuring existing assets or outputs are safely overwritten when forced.
- Overall, changes are targeted, maintain backward compatibility for interactive users, and unlock automation scenarios without altering default safety.

Use the context files to understand dependencies and generate a detailed step-by-step plan.

steps.operation: Update internal export orchestration to support force flag by updating watch_and_export
steps.reason: Centralizes overwrite behavior; if force is True, bypass user prompt and overwrite existing files; ensures all export subcommands share the same behavior
steps.side_effects: Adds a new boolean parameter to the function signature; may require updating all call sites and related tests; keeps default behavior interactive when force is False; introduces CI/CD friendly non-interactive path

steps.operation: Add --force flag to the HTML export command
steps.reason: Expose the force flag at the CLI level for HTML export; pass through to watch_and_export to allow overwriting in non-interactive environments
steps.side_effects: Updates CLI help text; requires tests to cover force vs non-force HTML export; ensures -f alias works for HTML export

steps.operation: Add --force flag to the Script export command
steps.reason: Expose the force flag at the CLI level for script export; pass through to watch_and_export
steps.side_effects: Updates CLI help text; requires tests to cover force vs non-force script export; ensures -f alias works for script export

steps.operation: Add --force flag to the Markdown (md) export command
steps.reason: Expose the force flag at the CLI level for md export; pass through to watch_and_export
steps.side_effects: Updates CLI help text; requires tests to cover force vs non-force md export; ensures -f alias works for md export

steps.operation: Add --force flag to the IPYNB export command
steps.reason: Expose the force flag at the CLI level for ipynb export; pass through to watch_and_export
steps.side_effects: Updates CLI help text; requires tests to cover force vs non-force ipynb export; ensures -f alias works for ipynb export

steps.operation: Add --force flag to the HTML_WASM export command
steps.reason: Expose the force flag at the CLI level for html_wasm export; pass through to watch_and_export
steps.side_effects: Updates CLI help text; requires tests to cover force vs non-force html_wasm export; ensures -f alias works for html_wasm export

step_plan.summary: Introduce a --force / -f flag across all marimo export subcommands (html, script, md, ipynb, html_wasm) to enable overwriting existing outputs without interactive prompts. Centralizes the behavior in watch_and_export while preserving the safe prompt for interactive users.
